message:
mcp-server v0.4 SOTA security stack: RBAC + JWT + rate limit + replay

Lays the security foundation requested ("fully implement all
capabilities including RBAC and auth, SOTA"). Four new modules
plus enforcement wiring through the planner.

## v0.4a — per-collection RBAC (the headline ask)

- New `[[allow]]` blocks in mcp.toml: backend (exact), collection
  (anchored regex), caps. Empty list = unrestricted (v0.3 compat).
- `allow.rs` — compile-and-check engine. Read is implicitly granted
  by any matching block; Publish/Admin require explicit caps grant.
- Anchored regex: `docs.*` matches `docs.public` but NOT
  `secret-docs.public` (hardens against prefix-match exploits).
- Planner threads `Capability` through `resolve_routes_for_cap`;
  refresh intent uses Publish, search uses Read.
- Refusals carry typed `code: "ALLOWLIST_DENIED_*"` per route in
  the decision trace — audit can tell "no rule" vs "rule but no cap".
- 4 unit tests + 1 e2e (allowed route works, denied route refuses
  with PolicyRefusedAllowlist + per-route refusal code).

## v0.4b — JWT bearer auth (OAuth-style scope→capability)

- New `JwtAuth` validator: jsonwebtoken signature + iss + aud + exp.
  RFC 8707 Resource Indicators enforced via aud claim.
- v0.4 ships HMAC (HS256/384/512); RSA/EC public-key keys + JWKS
  fetch loop land in v0.5 with the `use_pem` feature flag flip.
- Scope/scp claim → CapabilitySet via documented map:
    mcp:rulake:read     → Read
    mcp:rulake:publish  → Read + Publish
    mcp:rulake:admin    → Read + Publish + Admin
- Both `scope: "a b"` (space-separated string) and `scp: ["a","b"]`
  (array) forms accepted.
- 5 unit tests: valid token, wrong audience, expired (>leeway),
  wrong signature, scope-array form.

## v0.4c (foundation) — rate limit + replay modules

- `ratelimit.rs` — three governor buckets per ADR-004 §6:
  (transport, principal), (principal, backend, collection),
  (transport). Defaults match the ADR (60/120, 30/60, 600/1200).
  3 unit tests including the per-principal isolation case
  ("alice's hot collection cannot starve bob's").
- `replay.rs` — MCP-Request-Id LRU dedup over a 10k-entry window.
  Empty id (stdio's case) bypasses; second sighting inside window
  fails. 4 unit tests including post-eviction passes.
- Wiring into the HTTP middleware ships in v0.4d (next iter) —
  modules + tests are landed first so they're green and reviewable.

## Verification

- cargo build --release   → silent (1 unused-warning, harmless)
- cargo test --release    → 38/38 pass:
    19 unit (3 audit + 4 allow + 5 jwt + 4 replay + 3 ratelimit)
    12 smoke (incl. e2e RBAC denial test)
     7 HTTP e2e

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

 [[package]]
 name = "winnow"
 version = "0.7.15"

@@ -58,6 +58,18 @@ bytes = "1"
 # de-facto Rust idiom for timing-safe comparisons.
 subtle = "2"
 
+# Per-collection allow-list regex compilation (RBAC, ADR-004 §5).
+regex = { version = "1", default-features = false, features = ["std", "perf"] }
+
+# Layered rate limiting per ADR-004 §6 — (transport, principal),
+# (principal, backend, collection), (transport).
+governor = { version = "0.7", default-features = false, features = ["std", "quanta"] }
+dashmap = "6"
+
+# JWT (JWS) validation for `--auth jwt`. ADR-004 §5 SOTA path.
+# `default-features = false` keeps us off the OpenSSL backend.
+jsonwebtoken = { version = "9", default-features = false }
+
 # Async runtime used by rmcp's stdio transport.
 tokio = { version = "1.39", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros", "io-std", "signal", "sync", "net", "time"] }

@@ -32,6 +32,37 @@ pub struct McpConfig {
     /// planner's tie-break ("first registered wins" in v0.1).
     #[serde(default)]
     pub backends: Vec<BackendConfig>,
+
+    /// RBAC allow-list (ADR-004 §5). Empty = no per-collection
+    /// restriction (only the capability-tier gate fires). When
+    /// populated, every (backend, collection, capability) tuple
+    /// must match at least one block.
+    #[serde(default, rename = "allow")]
+    pub allow: Vec<AllowBlock>,
+}
+
+/// One [[allow]] block in mcp.toml.
+///
+/// ```toml
+/// [[allow]]
+/// backend    = "lake-prod"
+/// collection = "docs.*"      # regex
+/// caps       = ["read"]
+/// ```
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
+pub struct AllowBlock {
+    /// Backend id (exact match — backends are operator-controlled and
+    /// finite, regex is overkill).
+    pub backend: String,
+    /// Collection regex anchored implicitly (we wrap in `^…$` at
+    /// compile time so `docs.*` matches `docs`, `docs.public`, but
+    /// NOT `secret-docs.public`).
+    pub collection: String,
+    /// Capabilities granted by this block. `read` is always implied.
+    /// `["read", "publish"]` lets a principal call publish-tier tools
+    /// against this (backend, collection) pair.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub caps: Vec<String>,
 }
 
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Deserialize, Serialize)]
